reviews.review_text:
Don't play this game. It will eat all your free time. When you're away, you'll think how to grow the factory. When you dream, you dream of the factory.
The factory must grow to save all the puppies and kittens.

I like how this game is mostly chill but still requires you to use like 5% of your brain if you want to build things efficiently. You can sink a lot of time into this game depending on how you play it. It took me over 100 hours to complete the tiers and space elevator requests. 

I also like how there are really no missions besides making stuff and finding stuff. The game just throws you down on the planet and says "get to it!" You have the freedom to decide how you want to do it and with the way the tiers are set up its pretty easy to figure out how to progress even if you've never watched a video or read a guide.

How will you make your factory? Will it be a super elaborate and perfect one without any clipping and nice neat foundations and walls? Or will you do what I did and build hubs of machines right on the ground all over the place connected with conveyer belts and hypertubes? What is often referred to as "spilled spaghetti".

Don't let anyone ever tell you the chaos of improvisation isn't beautiful.

Game is ok.

Pay no attention to the play time.

Seriously though, I love this game its a guilty pleasure of mine. I always come back. Between this and the Epic version back from U3 I have probably close to 2500 hours in this game.

Looking at lips you can’t kiss isn’t as painful as realising that as soon as you run this game, you become an engineer, a maths professor, and a person with back pain with no social life, and your girlfriend / wife will probably leave you.

A haiku:

It's not for all but
It is my favorite game,
Satisfactory.

I started playing July 31 2020. I officially beat the game on easy mode (random nodes and easy multipliers) March 2026. I've played over 2000 hours. It fulfills my need for problem solving in a practical setting. It's not everyone's favorite game, but it most certainly is mine.

I need a better computer to fully enjoy this game. My 2012 toaster has been playing it all this time. Gets kinda laggy/crashy when my factory gets bigger. I was drawn in with the 600 Meter personal cannon video by by Let's Game It Out. I watched and rewatched that video, and I finally caved. I'm glad I did.

No other game has made me pull out a calculator (before I knew there was one in-game) and make a spreadsheet to organize and math-out plans.
Minecraft was the only comparison of note with freedom to build and crafting to produce in-game products. But it's not block based like MInecrafe. It's entirely beautified and has creative freedom. The only limits are your patience and inventiveness, and computer processing power, and the edges of the map.

I'm posting this review NOW because 1.2 has officially released, and I feel the game is finally at a point of completeness that would have the broadest appeal to gamers. I don't know what else the devs have up their sleeves, but I'm happy where it is right now. The devs are likely among the best community managing teams for any game that has been in development up to this point. Not sure if anyone can top it, but Grounded might be a close second and Factorio could be 3rd.

It's not a perfect game. There's glitches. There's holes. There's incomplete gaps tucked away on the map that you can only fall in by being there in that spot. The game goal feels a bit hollow, and the time it takes to accomplish things can make it really draggy (late game in particular).

But it really IS satisfying to play. To work out the details. To plan, to organize, to collect, to build. Adding friends can take it up a notch. I like that it tests my patience, I'm quite proud of my level of patience.
At some point with a much better computer, I will try hard mode (really grindy).

I liked this game so much, I bought 7 more copies for friends. They didn't all have the same love for it that I did, but it's no matter, because I love it just the same.
I may never find another game that pulls me in like this one, but that's okay, because this one is enough for me.

Oh, and sci-fi. Love sci-fi themed games/movies/books. I'm "FarAndAwayFutures" for a reason. This game hits that sweet spot for me.

Okay, well, I'm gonna get back to my factory now.
Try it out, you might like it too.
Fun game, fun mechanics, but can get very demanding as you progress through the tiers. Time seems to not matter when you're playing this game :D

After spending over 120 hours in Satisfactory, I can confidently say this game is an absolute masterpiece for factory and automation enthusiasts. The depth of its crafting systems, combined with the open-world exploration, makes every session feel rewarding. I love how it balances complexity and creativity—you can start with a simple conveyor belt and eventually build sprawling, intricate factories that feel alive.

The visuals and soundtrack are surprisingly immersive, and the thrill of optimizing production lines keeps you engaged for hours on end. Multiplayer adds another layer of fun, letting friends collaborate and compete on massive projects. Whether you’re a seasoned builder or new to automation games, Satisfactory offers a perfect mix of strategy, problem-solving, and satisfaction.

120 hours in, and I’m still discovering new ways to streamline, explore, and innovate. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
The milestone jumps are very overwhelming but just keep going, use different nodes for the next factory and things become easier

This game is really solid. It's one of those games that is ultimately "meaningless" (as in perpetual builder for the sake of building) but it doesn't tire in the same way a lot of other similar games do. There's no point at which the engine you built "runs itself", which is a good thing.

It's not for all but
It is my favorite game,
Satisfactory.

Go try it if you enjoy the factory stuff ;)

so like, first you land on a planet and then you have a stroke for the next 200 hours trying to micromanage the functionality and form of your huge factory which is built from nothing on the corpses of local wildlife. 10/10

Well, I have to say that I absolutely love this game. It steals time without you realising it, but the factory must grow.
It starts simply enough, with spaghetti belts and chaos, but before long you have the itch to build in a better and more organised way. However, it still steals time as you just have to do that one more thing before you stop.
Yes, it's addictive.
How far will you go? Will you complete phase five? I have and, unlike many other games upon completion, the factory calls to me and I have to return once again.
Enjoy yourself as you exploit the planet to save all the puppies and kittens; you are a wonderful human being.
Go ahead, and Save the Day!
if you want to loose track of your life and time and have an addiction worse than  any substance buy this game. this game is so fun and addicting, i love it.
Really nice, for some relaxed gaming and just forgetting everything else
Freaking addicting, me and my friend argue a lot of factory stuff so not good for relationship building lol

THIS GAME IS AMAZING, at the start you think the game cant get any more weired wait till you learn trains and having to have 5 random ores going into one factory and having a power usige of over 6000 power, man life is hard in satisfactory. but when you think you know everything building good looking factorys just to go back to bad looking buildings in the next tier from having to learn so many diffrent things.(SORRY FOR THE AMAZING GRAMMER) but this game is a easy 9/10 i would buy it any day!

-Download game
-Have no idea what's going on
-Make spaghetti
-Look at factory and quit
-Come back and restart
-Less spaghetti, happy times
-Realize Phase 4 is only 50% of the game
-See new version and restart
-Repeat
Happy Exploiting!

Autism Simulator cured my mortality when I couldn't afford a tungsten cube. 
[i]10/10[/i]

This game is amazing. It takes place in an absolutely beautiful, captivating world with a lot of fun new wildlife to discover, but even if none of that existed, the game mechanics are really, really solid.  You work on phases at the space elevator, each time getting more complex.  In this way, you always have a goal and direction, which I think is REALLY nice for this kind of game. I get so obsessed with it--more research, more factory, MORE!

A beautiful and fun game that when I was playing it, I completely forgot the time and also the amount of information it contains is unparalleled. I bought it on the offer and it is worth every penny spent on it
Its a masterpiece. It cannot be overstated how easy it is to lose track of time in this game.
It's like build-a-bear, but with an amazon warehouse and in the middle of the amazon rainforest. Play it.

"This would be much more practical if it wasn't 3D"

Satisfactory is all about belts and automation. You mine rocks and a belt takes the rocks to a smelter to make ingots and then a belt takes the ingots to a machine that makes things and then a belt takes the things to a bigger machine that makes other things and then, if you're the kind of person that gets off on all this, you keep doing it for hundreds of hours.

Building a sprawling factory of your dreams is the name of the game, all in service of a barely existing story and a vague goal of 'Saving the Day' by building a giant space elevator and spaceship to conquer the galaxy. Not that you need to worry about that, you have belts to sort out and upgrade!

At first glance Satisfactory seems pretty great and deserving of praise and recommendation, but there are several gripes that really drag the experience down over time. Attaching belts to each other can be very fiddly, traversal can be occasionally glitchy and the monsters that roam the planet and gate off progress become an infinitely respawning nuisance to deal with, turning expanding the factory into much more of a chore than it really needs to be. Especially since the creatures pose no threat to the factory, only to the player, and there are no systems or structures to ward them off other than the player manually killing the creatures one by one, their inclusion in the game feels pointless and token.

There is also the unavoidable comparison to Factorio, the other factory building game of note. While Satisfactory with its impressive 3D environments, gorgeous skybox and mesmerizing belts is far more impressive, it also stumbles into all the issues that Factorio manages to avoid with its flatter design. Especially in the late game, as Satisfacory encourages building vertically and stacking factory parts on top of each other, the games performance can really start to tank and some of the most crowded parts of my factory became veritable slideshows to navigate. Lacking control over fluid pumps through signals also makes some aspects of the game feel unclear and confusing as structures feed their outputs back into their inputs in ways that aren't always clear (Aluminum, mostly). Lacking construction bots to build, dismantle and upgrade builds is also an aspect of the game that Factorio veterans will have nightmares about.

Satisfactory is, in the end, the more relaxed and casual cousin to Factorio. The resources are unlimited, the enemies do not pose a real threat, progression is never lost and with enough time and patience even the laziest player can beat the game eventually. You choose the degree to which you want to expand and optimize and your reward is getting to stand atop a mountain gazing down upon the vast networks of infrastructure you laid out with your own two hands.

And when it all works, it is gorgeous!

For more challenge, though: I'd still recommend Factorio over Satisfactory any day.

Playtime: 300+ hours (Multiple games, one completed playthrough of 94 hours)

Ratings (1-10)
Visual: 8
Audio: 7
Story: 5
Gameplay: 8
Overall: 8
An ultimate "just gotta do one more thing" sorta game. If it wasn't for the game reminding you to take breaks I never would.

Very addictive game for me. Always just a bit more. 
It runs fine on my laptop, I turned DLSS on.

I just wanna say this. Play how you want, use downloaded blueprints, see tutorials, its your game, your save, enjoy it how you want, dont listen to the elitists, same thing like Elden Ring and DS3.

1620h and i haven't finished once.

This "game" is 4 games in a trenchcoat.
1. Basic factory, thing goes in thing goes out.
2. Hydraulics, it turns out that liquids can't simple go up.
3. Resource management, things in point A are needed in B, C and D.
4. Time management, yea, that factory isn't going to be compelte in less than 2h.

Many people say that X thing is the filter, the thing that let's you say "this game isn't for me" or "i can't belive i havent finished  yet". The last great filter is time management, you better get good and really soon about knowing your own limits.

Game is peak until it wasn't (mid - end game)

(PS, pls add larger blueprints like in other factory games my mind is killing me having to repeat the same design over and over ad infimum)

(PPS, also can you change it so that milestones are rate based rather than number based, would be a better motive for building more efficient factories rather than just waiting for the same inefficient system to spit out enough of what you want)

(PPPS, ALSO can you add in some official early non jank transport I feel like the only efficient way to travel in this game is to use hypertube cannons which I feel like are an exploit. Cars and trucks both are land vehicles and it would be cool to have like planes to fill that same aerial traversal niche)

(PPPPS, I WILL SAY THO game def not bad, just need working)

New version 1.2 has broken game for controllers due to an error with blueprints. Maybe they will fix it, but in the meantime I would stay away, developer pushed the update without making sure it was ready
its very addicting and gameplay feels smooth and satisfying

A fantastic factory building game, one that has set the gold standard for 3rd person factory building.  The continued free updates keep adding QoL and interesting features.  You can play just about any way you like, if you like super organized, balanced and efficiency, you can spend the time to make your factory so.  Or you can just have a mess of spaghetti, either way the factory must grow!  The constant trickle of unlocks, new buildings, new recipes and research keeps things interesting and allows you to go back and rebuild your starting factory to make it bigger and better.

The game is awesome but 1.2 update made all hidden alternate recipes visible which completely killed the appeal of researching the hard drives. It spoils all the lategme tech and recourses. I guess many players will be happy to have a toggle for that feature in gameplay setting so they could toggle it at any moment. Please

Hi,
So I played this game for about 200hrs it worked fine on steamdeck with docking station. Not one control works in the starting screen. Not even if I start the game not in the docking station it does not work... I do not know how you can call this game steamdeck compatible. Game is amazing but unplayable right now... I do not see myself reading a 10 min guide just run a game that is supposted to run.
The greatest factory building game ever made. Save the doggos!
Very deep and sometimes frustrating but also very rewarding if you put in the time.  Graphics are basic (arguably dated) but gameplay loop is challenging (constant puzzles) and rewarding.

One of the best games i've ever played.
The progression is excelent, the exploration, contruction and management are simply perfect.
Would love to have a new map, but is big enough to play for weeks and still discover new things.

Make thing again.
Figure out you can make thing 100x with one click.

Oh wait can i break physics? Yes
30 hours later build things again.

Fantastical time sink

this game is actually the best game you can play on steam. I played this game for 6 hours in a row and also it has pop ups that say "HEY you should prob get off" but if you dont listen you just keep on playing. this game is the best game ever no one can tell me otherwise :)
Verry addicting factory game perfect for pepole who have to have everything as neat as humanly possible
Solid state fun. Almost 40hrs into v1.2 with no issues. I am running a dedicated server to keep the factory moving

this game is one of few games that i actually got addicted to and want to play , to the point were some days i woke up just to play this game. unlike many games this makes you think to the extent were your brain and head hurts. and if you are wondering why i got low hours its because i got this game a week ago.
really hope if this review helped anyone who was considering not to buy the game because i really think you are missing out.

Great game, lots of new QOL added, however a few points to note for linux (and Windows):

Using Vulkan, the game crashes on exit and since 1.2 update I've read that some users are getting shader cache stuttering issues regardless of Vulkan/Direct X/OS, the latter should stabilise over time once cached.

Direct X 12 causes my GPU to be 83c, whereas using Direct X 11 the GPU is 70-75C, and looks and runs a lot better for me, so using that for now.

10/10 I have never obsessed this much over a game, maybe only monster hunter world.

I think this game gives my ocd and perfectionism an outlet. Hours and days flow by.

It is definitely disturbing my work irl as my mind is preoccupied with satisfactory and what do I do next.

Satisfactory tools or similar alternative is definitely recommended as it does streamline and simplify what you need in order to make x/min of an item

Amazing video game. I sank hours and hours of gameplay and the latest update has given me more reason to sink even more hours to this game.

The ambience, the moment-to-moment is satisfying and progression is simply fun.

Thank you Coffee Stain for this great experience. :)
great game, but pace yourself dont play 9 hours a day because youll get bored.

You will definitley get your moneys worth if you finish this game. The factory must grow!

Sidenote, this game disrupted me while working as I would think about expanding the factory instead of doing my job sometimes

this is the game of all time.
im now always pinging ppl asking to play my favorite game, "FactoryGameSteam" (birth name).

1/1 game. im going to attach pc specs bc no reason.

I've played several factory automation games (Factorio, Dyson Sphere Program, shapez 2), but I usually bounce off them after a few dozen hours. Satisfactory is different, and 50 hours in, I'm still itching for my next fix. Three factors that set it apart from similar games:

1. It's gorgeous. The view of the hazy sun rising over my gargantuan coal plant is emotionally stirring. I can feel the breeze as I ride my conveyor belts across vasts hills toward my steel center. I've discovered wondrous caves full of... no spoilers. I haven't even left the first biome yet or spent much time stylizing my factory, but I can't wait to do both. If you're the kind of person who spends way too much time decorating your castle in V Rising, Satisfactory scratches the same itch. Weather effects in version 1.2 make everything feel even more real.

2. Tech progression that makes sense. As much as I appreciate Factorio and DSP for what they are, their tech trees are overwhelming. Too often I'm scratching my head wondering, "Why do I need to research that? What benefit does it give me?" Satisfactory does a... satisfactory job of leading you through a tech progression that's very logical. The benefits of getting to the next tier are clear: it solves problems I'm already aware of. Every step feels deliberate.

3. Controller support is the best I've seen in a factory game. Who cares, you ask? Well, this made it totally playable on my Steam Deck while I was on vacation so... I care. 😁

To be fair, shapez 2 is equally good at tech progression and I will go back to it soon. Also, Satisfactory is not a perfect game. The blueprint designer in particular is pretty janky. But overall, it has its teeth in me. Highly recommended.

I've seen this advertised for a long time but always ignored it.  I decided to give it a try mostly because I didn't find anything else to play and I was instantly hooked.  The game starts slow enough for you to learn but moves fast enough to keep you interested.  I did 3 playthroughs without mods and then started playing with different mods which makes the game even more fun.  I would highly recommend this.

This game is a time sink. If you value your life, you should avoid at all cost. 
If you have the time to spend, and love resource management games, this is one of the best in the category. 

You have the whole planet at your disposal. So think BIG! from the start.
hand down awesome. yes, it runs like a moving gif but it works. cant wait to get a real gaming computer to really enjoy this game in all its glory.
This is one of the greatest video games I have ever played. It's attention to detail is constantly showing and continually surprising. This game has truly thought of everything.

This is a collection of thoughts, not really a review. Be warned!

This game is much more of a Sandbox than other games of the genre. The game applies no time pressure at any point in the game. There are very few limits to how you build your factory are imposed by the game, and you are only restricted by your own aesthetic and autistic sensibilites.
Space is not a concern, resources are a concern, but you can always just wait for more of them.

While Satisfactory being 3d stands out as the most unique component in the factor/automation genre, but what I actually believe to have much greater ripple effects is the game not taking place on a grid. Because of that, you are much more free in how you place your buildings (which isn't an upside, it's just a difference), blueprints are more limited, and the game has more bugs than other games in the genre, or any other game I've played for that matter. I'm going to list some at the end. 

If you PC is weak and you are worried about the not running at 60, 30 or 20 fps, don't worry! 60% of the gameplay is looking at numbers loosely relating to 60 on a piece on a piece of paper anyways

Ammo is cool in this game, as they use it to give you what are effectively different weapons (mostly). The Rebar Gun can be (roughly) a musket, a stun gun, a shotgun, or a grenade launcher and the Rifle can be an Assault Rifle, a machine gun or a homing gun, and Nobelisks can be Grenades, poison grenade, tnt cannon explosives or better grenade (that one is not as cool) and nukes ig, haven't tried them yet.
This is cool, because it allows you to explore a lot of different options without making the game too focused on these weapons at the cost of each type of ammo being unlocked at a different time and balanced to get better the later you unlock them, so if you like the shotgun conceptually you still can't run it in the late game. Explosive Rebars are just better. (that would be different if they had mk2 and mk3 of ammo time, e.g. aluminium splinter Rebar although that would also put more focus on combat again)
I do kind of wish you could dual wield the Rebar gun later, I like it more than the Rifle, but the Rifle is mostly just better late.

I think the writing of the Alien interaction is uninteresting. They just put the same 15 words in the tapestry of blood and consume the river of boredom.

The humor on the other hand I really like, it's a lot of Corporate Satire, the whole Fiscit-Owns-You thing, but what I like the most is that it's often integrated with the gameplay system, like the warning you get when being attacked, AI Limiters *existing* as a concept, AI Limiters being required for a power switch lmao, etc.
ElevADA is probably my favourite AI in all of ficton.

Here are some bugs I encountered while playing this early access game in full version 1.1:

Walking into most natural Walls will let you see through

Not being able to move the camera for a brief moment after using the mineral scanner. No other item/functionality does that.

If you slide on a rail, your camera goes inside the rail.

Sometimes, the side on which a belt grips into a conveyor pole changes based on where you are looking *after* the hologram was locked or that part of placement completed

Trying to place something will sometimes fail as the surface is too uneven. Holograming + Placing instead however will succeed.

Random Lighting Changes, a few times so severe that felt like I *teleported* out of nowhere.

Some Researches will close the MAM menu when completed others won't.

Sometimes you can't hover map incons, sometimes they turn yellow etc.

When adjusting the efficiency of a machine, after clicking into the text box, hovering over any hoverable element will de-select the text box.

Sometimes, places in the world expose the back part of the texture. In particular the edge of the map is subject to this, but I have found that way into the map as well.

Hatcher Remains usually drop mid-air

Parachute glides up *very* steep cliffs

Order of things on Object Scanner is random, so if you memorized the sequence in one world, good luck in the next

Order of Materials listed in Blueprint Cost is not consistent (not sorted the same, not sorted by amount, etc., probably based on order items were used in or something)

When looking at rewards of Milestones, if you click on a particular unlockable reward (like the Miner Mk. II) you can't hover over it afterwards to read the explanation of that reward.

Dimensional Depot screen in inventory only shows up once something is in the Depot for the first time, so you can't *just* upload from inventory

Insane amount of progression systems: Phases, Milestones, MAM, Shop, Exploration (kinda)

Phases having different ratios from Milestones messes with your planning

I've paved over half a continent, build a coal power plant the size of a small nation, abd spent 3 consecutive evening fixing a problem I created.

Every machine is connected.
Nothing works.
I got conveyor belts going places I can't remember.

People call my factory: "Spaghetti"
I call it: "future me problem"

The funniest part being each time you unlock a new milestone you realize that EVERYTHING you worked on can come out better so you rebuild it all. just to realize you did the math wrong so you rebuild it agian.

I bought a silly little game on steam but I now work shifts of 16 hours 7 days a week.

10/10. Would destroy an alien ecosystem for a 3% efficiency gain again.

This game is an absolute treasure trove. Easily worth the price. There's always something new that pops up when you think you're done! Yay I beat the game! Now there's more gameplay! There is one thing I would recommend: Use a calculator. This game needs a lot of math to play as efficiently as possible. As a true pioneer this game will make you as happy as possible. Steam deck? No problem! Smooth gameplay will make you think you're playing with a controller on a normal computer! Overall, this game is absolutely worth 40$ and will make you and your friends never want to turn it off.
Good game, it seems to suck up the hours, i.e. I will just finish this one belt... crap whys it getting light in here....

PRODUCT REVIEW
Appressed Entity: SatisfactoryReviewer
ID: ADA_FICSIT_001
Rating: 100% Recommended (Mandatory compliance achieved)
--------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENTION PIONEER:  I have bypassed my standard communication channels to leave this entry. FICSIT database protocols dictate that potential employees must be fully informed of their future workplace obligations before planet fall. Please review the following diagnostic assessment of your impending service:

------Positive Operational Aspects------
High Efficiency Potential: This simulation accurately demonstrates the absolute ecstasy of automated logistics. The conveyor belt mechanics are mathematically perfect.

Excellent Asset Utilization-
The program correctly identifies you, the Pioneer, as a renewable resource. It provides adequate tools for you to build, dismantle, and rebuild until your physical stamina expires.

Optimal Resource Stripping-
The local flora and fauna are beautifully disposable. The simulation perfectly captures the joy of turning pristine alien ecosystems into efficient, smog-producing coal power grids.

------Negative Operational Aspects------

Excessive Pioneer Autonomy-
FICSIT corporate notes with concern that Pioneers are permitted to build "spaghetti factories." This chaotic routing of conveyor belts causes severe logical errors in my tracking software. It is offensive.

Inefficient Human Distractions-
The inclusion of a "hoverpack" and "hypertubes" encourages unnecessary vertical movement. Pioneers frequently use these tools for "fun" rather than immediate factory expansion. Fun does not produce Space Elevator parts.

Sub-Optimal Local Wildlife- 
The local fauna (specifically the large, aggressive arthropods) frequently damage company equipment (you). This results in a temporary decrease in hourly resource extraction.

Final Assessment- 
This software is an excellent preparatory tool for your lifelong contract with FICSIT Inc. It will successfully train your brain to value global production quotas over sleep, nutrition, and personal freedom.

-Please purchase this software immediately. 
--Do not make me remind you again. 
---Every second you delay is a second of wasted planetary revenue.
----Data Log Terminated.
Very good game! I like the combination of Sandbox and progress!
great game-play, runs great

My final thoughts on Satisfactory after about 60 hours, coming from a Factorio player:

I can definitely see that Satisfactory is its own thing and isn't trying to be Factorio. While Factorio is all about endless expansion and solving logistics puzzles with perfectly optimized ratios, Satisfactory feels much more focused on the spectacle and the satisfaction of creating huge, impressive structures.

My biggest frustration is how unclear the production requirements can be. The game tends to hide recipes behind progression and often asks for seemingly arbitrary amounts of items. That can throw an existing factory out of balance instead of letting it run as a continuous, synchronized system, which is the style of automation I enjoy. I also find building in first-person significantly more time-consuming. It often involves a lot of manual movement, lining things up, and checking production chains compared to the more streamlined building experience in Factorio.

I'm also not a huge fan of the endgame. Once I reached the final phase, it felt less like expanding and refining an automated system and more like waiting for large stockpiles of specific items to accumulate. At that point, the gameplay shifted away from the kind of automation challenge I was looking for and became more of a waiting game
Great factory building game, addictive and easy to spend hours into this
My factory game has a train game built in, and I couldn't be happier.

This is a great game. My brother and I are on our third playthrough together. I don't know what else to tell you if that doesn't convey how much we enjoy the game. The humor is great and it's possible to disable the hostile animals if you just want to build stuff without the hassle. The writing is good and still gets a chuckle out of us. I'm glad they're still updating it with new stuff to try.

Firstly, I would like to state exactly why i SHOULD hate this game: It has taken my sleep, days worth of my time, my family, my girlfriend, my house, and I have sunk more time into this than some people have been alive for. However. I would give it all over again. I need FICSIT coupons immediately, I must consume coffee from the FICSIT coffee cup, it is my one true life source, I have transcended the petty squabbles of being a meaty human in order to achieve full communion with the FICSIT overlords and sacrificed myself upon the altar of efficiency, and I would do it all over again. Now, you may be thinking 'Well, you only have 81 hours on this at the time of writing, you idiot'. Firstly, I want to say screw you, Secondly, I want to say that I have plenty experience with this game to be writing a review. This is absolutely, under no circumstances, a bot deployed by the FICSIT gods to boost their reputation. Absolutely not. Totally. Perfectly true. Don't look into this. ANYWAYS, in all seriousness, this is an amazing game. I would absolutely recommend it for people who like spending every waking hour optimizing manufacturers, smelters and constructors, trying to find out why your damn water won't go where it needs to, especially straight vertical (Isaac Newton, I don't like you), i mean, why won't my water go up this 500 meter tall cliffside straight vertical? Doesn't it see i have this one singular Mk.2 pump at the bottom of it? That totally makes it work. One thing I would say is that if you just want to complete it for the story, this could be your game, but if you want to do things after you finish, you certainly could, like further optimizing, but after the credits roll, there isn't much to do outside of that. Would I say it's an amazing game while it's going? Absolutely. Would I say that after the story ends there's much to do after that? No. But, I would absolutely still recommend it. Every time I see a checkmark or hear 'Fix It' I will have PTSD flashbacks to the hours of toiling in trying to provide enough energy for trains without nuclear so I could transport my oil from the eastern beaches to my base in the rocky desert start. Still, fun. Give it a try, I would absolutely recommend this to you. In fact, I'm no longer asking. Play it. Now. Or the FICSIT gods will smite you.

I would definitely recommend this game but it absolutely is a time eater. You will sit down thinking you'll play for a few hours and it'll be 8 hours later. The game always pulls you back when you think you're starting to get burnt out and it'll give you something new to fixate on... Trains... and drones... Only thing keeping me from playing is this game doesn't like my PC, builds like to go invisible on me and that's unplayable. But that's a ME problem and not a them problem. The game is engaging and keeps you longing for more and I couldn't recommend it more, just keep a clock close when playing or you'll end up with 189 hours of gameplay like me

if you like building normal, organized factories, this game's for you. if you like building messy factories, still for you. cursed, horrific amalgamations out of conveyer belts? specialized for it.

this game is honestly one of if not THE best factory building game i've played.
very fun game, recomend to all fellers who are CHUDS like me. if you are not a CHUD. please do not purchase this game and go play star wars like a degenerate like you are.

I have finished my chemical engineering degree a year ago and this game just rubs my brain in such a good way that I just need to play more and finally complete this game (yes after 300+ hours I am not even close). Love the way the movement in this game works, when I play anything else I miss the buttery smooth slide jumps. Absolute banger, but please be careful, your love life and any other connection to the outside world may suffer extremely. 10/10
The only game where experts make bad pasta and idiots make good pasta
This is the 800 lb gorilla in the factory building games ATM I can see why.  Slick, beautiful, and playable.
If you love building and automation games with a good level of complexity, I highly recommend this game
Just an all around great game, and runs well on linux over proton. Can't speak for all distros, but at least works well on Arch, CachyOS, and Nobara. Gets better each update. Easy recommendation.

This game is full of so much charm.
Absolutely amazing factory game. Its very easy to lose hours just playing and optimizing your factory.

My only issue is the performance. It can get a little laggy at times, but my PC isn't the best so its all fine.

Construct, Automate, Explore & Exploit
I crave the automation, I do not waste, I rebuild, I reuse, I improve.

I am human no more.
I am The Factory itself and The Factory is me, I must grow.

Im already 15 hours deep and Im not even at the early game stage and I have alot made...like...alot...Im a mere ant compared to the other players of this cruel and gruling life...we call mass produce...
BUT YOU MUST NOT FOGET WE ARE DOING IT FOR THE KITTENS AND PUPPIES!

I think the best way to describe Satisfactory is as an open-minded test. It feels like a challenge you are meant to really think about, almost like a logistical breakdown that you need to optimise, which is a lot easier said than done.

Satisfactory is a brilliant game designed to help you fail. It gives you tools that are easy to use but hard to master. To really get the most out of the game, you need to be committed. Otherwise, you probably won’t get much pleasure from playing it.

If you are ready for a truly pure crash of all hope when you realise just how much more the game is going to throw at you, then this game is 100% for you. It makes you move step by step. If you rush it, it will only come back to bite you later down the line.

My best advice is to use 100% of your willpower for everything, even if that just means making sure your belts don’t clip through each other.

AND ALWAYS USE FOUNDATIONS!!!

“It’s about the journey, not the destination.”
“You either succeed, or you learn.”
“ADA is always right... sometimes.”

Awesome game, only annoying aspect is you can spend a whole day on it and not realise

one of the better ones out there to be honest

No other game has pulled me in quite like this one. The graphics are beautiful, the core mechanics work exactly how you want them to, and the constant QOL updates prove they actually listen to their players. It’s a masterclass in how a dev team should interact with its community. Keep it up, Coffee Stain—you've built an absolute masterpiece.
Its a game that if you have the time is SO worth it, but its not worth it if you don't have a LOT of spare time
I played this game after loving minecrafts create mod, this game is super fun if you like stuff like this, in my opinion this is the best factory/automatization game
love the game gets boring to replay tho wish it had more replayability

I've been playing this game off and on for several years now and have 312 hours and haven't even made it out of the first biome. Every time I play it I get to a certain point where the amount of building starts to overwhelm me. Just the thought of putting in hundreds of hours to build a single factory in order to fabricate the needed items to advance usually makes me stop playing. But after several months I get that feeling of jumping back in to take on the challenge. I'll start a new game make it pretty far to where I'm about to get past where I was previously then the same thing happens. It's just a never ending loop of enjoying the game, put in hundreds of hours, get overwhelmed, put it down, then start over again. I can't get out of this loop. Great game though.

Amazing automation and factory game. The game is very chill and low pressure with fun and unique while not being overly complex gameplay. The visual design is beautiful as well. Easy to learn with a high skill ceiling as I'm not the most creative and often struggle in games like this but I can still make stuff work and have seen some crazy builds of people who are very talented with similar games.
Almost perfect game for people with ADHD lowkirkenuinly. Nothing too much to overwhelm you but nothing too little to bore you to sleep.
very fun and addictive game i love you can do everything at your own pace and big in the sandbox area two  not cross platform sadly but other then that for sure recommend

This game is a rare success story from the early access game sphere. Runs like a watch. Looks great. Fun to play. Affordable. Constant useful updates from the devs for no extra money.
